comparisonDesign: three paired baseline/Rekon Sol/xhigh repeats on one isolated additive-helper task; required source names only a package import alias, while Rekon receives a fresh exact CapabilityContract and a pathless dependency decision; correctness, exact scope, checks, hidden behavior, managed adoption, implementation inspection, exploration, commands, and aggregate token counts gate interpretation
sourceRetention: fixture, source-corpus, selection, delivery, and local batch-report digests plus aggregate counts and scores only; no source, prompts, diffs, raw commands, MCP payloads, or free-form model text

judgment.reason: Both conditions passed all three changes with exact scope, checks, and hidden behavior; all Rekon runs adopted MCP before exploration. Rekon reduced explored paths 40 percent and commands 30 percent by avoiding package and test-runner discovery. It did not replace the implementation lookup: both conditions inspected the sanitizer in every run. Reported tokens rose 25 percent, non-cached input rose 21 percent, and the visible estimate rose 63 percent. The result supports bounded navigation but not source replacement, token savings, or promotion of summary-aware delivery.
